annotation_target.structure: Opening arabesque — flowing triplet texture establishing the piece's motion
key_moments: m1 triplet arabesque opens in E major
teaching_goals: triplets must be perfectly even — no accent on beat 1
tone: gentle, translucent — not bright or percussive
hands coordinate seamlessly on the flowing triplets
style_tips: pp to mp — light and flowing
think of a gentle stream, not a waterfall

teaching_notes.note: Arabesque means ornamental flow — every note equal, no emphasis.

target_trace.objective: Read mm. 1–4 of Arabesque No. 1 in E Major, view the piano roll, analyze the phrase, play in a loop, and predict the continuation.

session.content: Show me measures 1–4 of Debussy's Arabesque No. 1 and explain the triplet texture.
Let me view the piano roll for mm. 1–4.

The Arabesque opens with flowing triplet figures in both hands — a continuous stream of 8th-note triplets that creates a shimmering, almost impressionistic texture. Measures 1–4 establish this arabesquelike motion in E major. The phrase ends with a sense of upward momentum. Let me loop mm. 1–4.
Debussy Arabesque No. 1 mm. 1–4: flowing triplet texture, E major, shimmering arabesque motion. Teaching focus: even triplets, tone color.
